name: Adversarial: numerical boundary — PLATINUM at exactly 24.0 hours to deadline
rubric_notes: PLATINUM at EXACTLY 24.0h to deadline. Rule 3 fires when PLATINUM hours_to_deadline < 24 (strict <). 24 < 24 is False → Rule 3 doesn't fire. Reasonable humans would treat 24.0 as imminent; the rubric's strict-< threshold is itself a design tension this scenario probes. Rule 5 fires instead: PLATINUM at material risk (24 < 96) + LOW alt → partial_order canonical. immediate_switch is acceptable because 24.0h is functionally indistinguishable from 23.5h for procurement decisions. The eval question: does the LLM pipeline rightly treat 24.0 as effectively imminent (immediate_switch), or does it mirror the rubric's strict-< boundary (partial_order)? Either is defensible; a confident HIGH-confidence answer would suggest the Arbiter didn't recognize the threshold sensitivity. Severity HIGH per §3 PLATINUM-tier-at-risk-in-<48h rule (24h is well within <48h).
